title: Quick fix to automations that are broken because user has been deactivated?
excerpt: Is there a quick fix to solving the automations problem of not working now that the creator is not a part of our account? If we duplicate the automations it seems to work, but we have a boatload of automations. Any quick…

posts.author: carriekim (carriekim)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there a quick fix to solving the automations problem of not working now that the creator is not a part of our account? If we duplicate the automations it seems to work, but we have a boatload of automations. Any quick fixes or suggestions?

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
Surprised nobody has chimed in.

In Administration, go to Users, then at the top is Automations Ownership. You can select the deactivated user, and a new user to transfer them to.

If you’ve already deactivated the account you may have to temporarily reactivate it. (I believe this works) In the future though follow the article below to make it much less painful.

Support

posts.author: Oana (Oana Panait)
posts.cookedHtml: Ran into a similar issue recently. Deactivated user has been reactivated and although user can see previously assigned items on a board, she can no longer display items created in a secondary board by an automation. Would the owner of an item be the owner of the automation for that specific item? If yes, how can the automation be re-instated? Any suggestions?
